name: The interior of Vinalopo Medio — Circular from Monovar
name_original: El interior del Vinalopó Medio — Circular desde Monóvar
region_intro: Road cycling around Elda, located in the interior of Alicante province, offers diverse landscapes and varied terrain. The region is characterized by craggy, mountainous areas with peaks exceeding 1,000 meters, providing a range of gradients for cyclists. Mediterranean forests and natural parks like Serra del Maigmó contribute to the scenic backdrop. This topography supports both gentle stretches and challenging climbs. Best road cycling routes around Elda The most popular road cycling route is Tour around the Protected Landscape of Serra del Maigmó and El Cid, a 46.1 miles (74.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 39 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the natural park's varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is The interior of Vinalopo Medio — Circular from Monovar, a moderate 41.6 miles (67.0 km) path. This route offers a circular journey through the Vinalopo Medio region, featuring diverse scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Castle and Hermitage of San Pedro – Agost to Maigmó Road loop from Monóvar, a 52.7 miles (84.9 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes and past historic sites, often completed in about 3 hours 57 minutes. Road cycling around Elda is defined by mountainous terrain, Mediterranean forests, and historic settlements. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging ascents. The routes in Elda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 3000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Elda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Elda?
A: There are over 80 road cycling routes around Elda, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The region's diverse terrain ensures there's always a new adventure to discover.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling near Elda?
A: Elda, situated in the interior of Alicante province, is characterized by craggy, mountainous terrain with peaks exceeding 1,000 meters. This provides a varied topography, from gentle stretches through Mediterranean forests to challenging climbs. You'll find routes that offer significant elevation changes, making it an appealing destination for cyclists seeking diverse gradients.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, while Elda is known for its challenging terrain, there are 5 easy road cycling routes available. These routes offer a more relaxed experience, allowing you to enjoy the scenic landscapes without overly strenuous climbs. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e892151850/the-interior-of-vinalopo-medio-circular-from-monovar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The interior of Vinalopo Medio — Circular from Monovar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option that provides diverse scenery.

Q: What are some of the more challenging road cycling routes around Elda?
A: For experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge, Elda offers 29 difficult routes. These often feature significant elevation gains and cover longer distances. A notable difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e890917265/tour-around-the-protected-landscape-of-serra-del-maigmo-and-el-cid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour around the Protected Landscape of Serra del Maigmó and El Cid&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the natural park's varied and demanding terrain.

Q: What natural landmarks or scenic viewpoints can I discover along the road cycling routes?
A: The region around Elda is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter impressive summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3072247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Silla del Cid Summit&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/586966" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola summit&lt;/a&gt;, which offer panoramic views. Routes often pass through Mediterranean forests and near natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3526393" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almortxó Sand Dunes&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6468510" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Clot of the Black Waters&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3867106" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Elda from El Bolón&lt;/a&gt; is another popular viewpoint.

Q: Are there any historical sites or attractions to see while road cycling in the area?
A: Yes, Elda's surroundings are dotted with historical sites. You might cycle past the imposing 12th-century Castle of Sax or the extraordinary historic agricultural-industrial settlement of Colonia de Santa Eulalia. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39559595" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colonia de Santa Eulalia – Sax Fortress loop from Elda-Petrer&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlight these historical points of interest.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available around Elda?
A: Many of the road cycling routes around Elda are circular, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1386562" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle and Hermitage of San Pedro – Agost to Maigmó Road loop from Monóvar&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic loop through mountainous landscapes and past historic sites.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Elda?
A: Elda benefits from an appealing climate, making it suitable for road cycling for much of the year. The broader Alicante province is popular with professional cyclists due to its ideal climate. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for long rides, while winter can also be pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Elda?
A: The road cycling routes in Elda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which includes both challenging climbs and scenic stretches through Mediterranean forests, as well as the opportunity to explore historic settlements and natural parks.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Elda?
A: While many routes in Elda feature significant elevation, there are 5 easy routes that could be more suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride. These routes often utilize quieter roads, providing a safer and more enjoyable experience for all ages. The Vía Verde del Maigmó, a converted railway line, is also a popular choice for recreational cycling, though its suitability for road bikes depends on the specific section and rider preference.

Q: Can I find road cycling routes that pass through or near towns for refreshment stops?
A: Yes, many routes in the Elda region connect or pass near towns and villages like Monóvar, Petrer, and Sax. This allows for convenient stops at local cafes or restaurants. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31406119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Petrer – Monóvar loop from Elda-Petrer&lt;/a&gt; offers opportunities to explore these charming settlements.
tips: Very quiet and picturesque stretch of road with very nice views of the Sierre de Cofer. The slope is very gentle and the surface is quite rough in some sections, be especially careful if you take it downhill.
